PHASE:        Phase 8 Batch 2 Item 4 SS-3 — Alert Rules Export feature work complete on feat/exports-alert-rules branch. Third per-entity export endpoint built: /api/exports/alert-rules GET handler with manual auth, upload rate-limit tier, 10001-take overflow → HTTP 413, sha256 filterHash, CSV/PDF branches with creator include + 9-column AlertRuleRow (stringified conditionJson + comma-joined notificationChannels), DATA_EXPORT audit. UI buttons inserted on /alerts page header before View History + New Rule. Acceptance gate fully GREEN. Awaiting squash-merge approval (matches the SS-1/SS-2 ship cadence: commit on branch → user confirms → squash-merge → STATE chore → push → branch delete → final-ship STATE chore). Prior same-day shipped to origin: SS-2 Patrols Export (52869ae + 72adb33 + e735686), SS-1 Events Export (0171309 + 3ed3382 + 1c783cd), SS-0 Export Foundation (4caa449), Alert History Log (7da0cab), Notification.patrolId FK (895fa84).

LAST_DONE:    Phase 8 Batch 2 Item 4 SS-3 — Alert Rules Export feature work committed (or ready to commit) on feat/exports-alert-rules branch.
              Files added (apps/web/src/app/api/exports/alert-rules/):
              - `route.ts`: GET Route Handler with `// Non-tRPC: manual auth required` header (security.md L11). Flow: requireRouteAuth() → rateLimiters.upload.check(userId) (20/min) → coerce query-string `isActive` ("true"/"false"/undefined) → boolean before safeParse → alertRuleListFilters.safeParse({isActive}) → prisma.tenant.findUniqueOrThrow for slug+name → prisma.alertRule.findMany with `where: {tenantId, ...isActive}` + `include: { creator: { select: { id, fullName } } }` + `take: 10001` for overflow detection → HTTP 413 JSON if length > 10000 → flatten to typed AlertRuleRow (9 columns: id, name, condition=JSON.stringify(conditionJson), channels=notificationChannels.join(", "), isActive="true"/"false", creatorName=creator.fullName, creatorId=createdBy, createdAt ISO, updatedAt ISO) → sha256 filterHash from sorted-keys JSON.stringify(filters) → branch on format=csv|pdf → toCsv() or renderExportPdf() → writeExportAudit() AFTER success with filterHash + rowCount → NextResponse with Content-Type/Content-Disposition/Cache-Control: no-store. PDF Buffer wrapped in Uint8Array for NextResponse. Pattern identical to SS-1/SS-2.
              - `__tests__/route.test.ts`: 6 tests — CSV byte-level BOM (carrying SS-1 🟡 lesson; assertions use `new Uint8Array(await res.arrayBuffer())`) + tenant-scoped findMany + assertion that CSV body contains `in_app, email` joined channels + escaped-JSON conditionJson (`""priority"":200`), PDF content-type, 401 missing session, 413 when findMany returns 10001 rows + no audit row written, DATA_EXPORT audit shape with 64-char-hex filterHash + entityType="alert-rules" + changesJson={format,rowCount}, isActive filter propagation into prisma.where with boolean coercion verified. Mocks @/server/auth, @marine-guardian/db (alertRule/tenant/auditLog), @react-pdf/renderer's renderToBuffer (spread retains the React components needed by export-pdf.tsx at module-load).

              Files modified:
              - `apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/alertRule.ts`: extracted `alertRuleListFilters = z.object({ isActive: z.boolean().optional() })` as a new top-level export. `alertRuleRouter.list` now uses `alertRuleListFilters.extend({cursor, limit})` for its input. Mirrors the SS-1 eventListFilters and SS-2 patrolListFilters extracts — single source of truth for the Route Handler to reuse. SS-4 router refactors (notificationListFilters + alertHistoryListFilters) will follow the same pattern.
              - `apps/web/src/app/(dashboard)/alerts/page.tsx`: added buildExportUrl import; inserted 2 Export buttons (`<Button asChild variant="outline" size="sm">` wrapping `<a href={buildExportUrl("alert-rules", {}, format)} download>`) inside the existing `<div className="flex items-center gap-2">` header right-side group at L172, positioned BEFORE the existing View History + New Rule buttons. Same `<a download>` shape as the events and patrols pages (Next.js Link does not honor `download`).
              - `docs/CHANGELOG_AI.md`: new SS-3 entry prepended above SS-2.
              - `docs/IMPLEMENTATION_MAP.md`: Last updated line refreshed; SS-3 item flipped from `[ ]` to `[x]` with full SS-3 details; Next Step section rewritten with SS-4 as the only remaining export sub-session; "Shipped since" list adds the SS-3 entry alongside the SS-2 final-ship marker.

              Schema/migrations: none — SS-3 is application code only.

              Acceptance gate (all GREEN):
              - typecheck 6/6 packages clean
              - lint 5/5 packages clean (one trivial fixup mid-session: removed unnecessary `?.` + `??` on `r.creator.fullName` since the Prisma include with a required FK relation produces a non-null creator type — eslint correctly flagged the optional chain as unnecessary; simplified to `r.creator.fullName` with no test impact)
              - tests: 72/72 in apps/web (was 66 — added 6 alert-rules route handler tests) + 7/7 in packages/jobs (cached, unchanged) = 79 total
              - `pnpm --filter @marine-guardian/web build` — succeeds. Next.js registers `/api/exports/alert-rules` as ƒ Dynamic Route (154 B / 103 kB) alongside `/api/exports/events` and `/api/exports/patrols`.
              - TDD order intact: wrote test file first → verified RED (module-not-found on ../route) → implemented → verified 6/6 GREEN on first test run (lint fixup was pure type-narrowing, no test impact).

              EXECUTION NOTES:
              Per the documented hook-injection thrashing lesson, stayed Opus-direct throughout. Hook injection still firing this session (vercel-plugin "vercel-functions"/"next-cache-components"/"nextjs"/"next-forge" suggestions on Read+Write+Edit; context-mode observation pointers on every Read; claude-mem timeline pointers; PostToolUse-validate suggestions on the new route file for "observability instrumentation" — rejected, matches SS-1/SS-2 instrumentation surface i.e. none). Opus 4.7 absorbed all of them cleanly. No plan deviations of substance — SS-3 followed the SS-2 pattern exactly with the documented additions for the array + JSON column formatters.

              Plan deviations (2, minor):
              (1) Used `JSON.stringify(conditionJson)` for the condition column over the plan-file's `condition: "priority>=200"` example. Reason: conditionJson is an opaque Json column whose internal shape varies between rules; the raw JSON is the honest representation, and downstream consumers can parse if they need a structured view. Channels formatter is `.join(", ")` matching the plan example.
              (2) Column count is 9 (one fewer than SS-1/SS-2's 10). AlertRule is a leaner entity than Event/Patrol; padding to 10 with synthetic columns would be artificial. Visual parity in the PDF table is not worth a fake column.
